citation_and_locator: Matthews, Stephen and Virginia Yip. 2011. Cantonese: A Comprehensive Grammar, 2nd ed.; CUHK companion website, chapter 5.1 Personal pronouns
what_it_supports: Direct singular/plural paradigm `ngo5/ngo5dei6`, `nei5/nei5dei6`, and `keoi5/keoi5dei6`, classified as personal pronouns.
limit: Paradigm does not by itself test productive attachment to names or common human nouns.

citation_and_locator: Chan, Brian Hok-Shing. 1999. Classifiers, Demonstratives and Classifier-to-Demonstrative Movement. UCL Working Papers in Linguistics 11; p. 240, ex. (21) and n. 18
what_it_supports: Contrasts accepted `keoi5 dei6`, speaker-limited/marginal `Siu2-ming4 dei6`, rejected common-noun `lou5baan2 dei6`, and preferred associative `Siu2-ming4 keoi5 dei6`; concludes `dei6` is not a full general plural marker.
limit: Older judgments and informal speaker observation require modern replication; name-plus-marker profile is associative, not ordinary plurality.

what_it_supports: Restricts the form to personal pronouns or `jan4`; lists `ngo5dei6`, `nei5dei6`, `keoi5dei6`, and lexical `jan4dei6` “other people.”
limit: Lexicographic entry does not provide acceptability contrasts or prove that `jan4dei6` is compositionally identical to the ordinary pronoun paradigm.

citation_and_locator: Lui, Pun Ho. 2026. The associative plural in Cantonese. Language and Linguistics 27(2):228-256. DOI 10.1075/lali.00256.lui
what_it_supports: Direct `X + keoi5dei6` associative construction; bound `dei6` is a less natural and rarely attested modern alternative; distinguishes associative plurality from coordination and other group readings.
limit: Does not license bound `dei6` as ordinary plural morphology on common human nouns; full restriction inventory remains under UC-RQ-009.

citation_and_locator: Tam, Hugo Wing-Yu. 2021. Development of the Cantonese Grammar Profile: Reconsidering Pedagogical Sequence of Grammar Items in Cantonese as a Second Language. Tsing Hua Journal of Chinese Studies 51(3):539-585
what_it_supports: Confirms the cited grammar-profile source exists and concerns pedagogical inventory design.
limit: Checked metadata does not expose a direct `dei6` boundary analysis; not used as core evidence for this disposition.
